text: Today we will talk about how to make dynamic routes in Next.js and we will also see how to customize 404 page Like this video and write Next.js in the comment section Let's go to the computer screen Let's roll the intro Alright guys, now we will see about dynamic routes So I will right click and create a new app and I will run the same command and I will name my project as dynamic routes and I will make my project by selecting default So what are dynamic routes? I will tell you about it See, whenever you want to do anything about dynamic routes related to your project I will say come on documentation because Next.js keeps changing things I am telling you that Next.js is running 14 now 15 will also come, maybe 21 will also come The concept will be the same, the syntax can be slightly different So now I have made it into JavaScript and what I will do here is I will make a page Like I am making a blog Let's assume I am making a blog I will do cd here and I will do dynamic routes here and after this I will do code. So that it opens in my VS code and I run my server by running npm run dev and after running my server my app looks like this and our home page of Next.js 14 will be visible Nothing new in that Assume I am making a blog I will have a page with the name blog so it will be blog.js All blogs will come here and I will write all blogs All my blogs will come here Now any particular blog If I want to show a particular blog So what I will do is I will say blog post slash and I will make a slug here like this and slash page.js So see what I did here slash blog post slash slug This slug is a variable I have written it in square bracket So what I will do is I will export this page This is a react functional component and here I will say params.slug So if I come here and I say slash blog post slash harry So it will give me my post harry If I say js So it will give me js If I say python Which means that I can access a variable from my url Then what I can do here I can fetch your blog by its slug I can fetch it So I can do this kind of work If I am using dynamic routes Now assume I want to make 50 blog posts Suppose my database has 50 blog posts One on python, one on java, one on javascript and so on So will I make routes for all of them one by one? No I will make dynamic routes I will say a variable will come after blog post What will that variable be? That will be known here So I will write here slash blog post slash python and python will be available in my page I can say that if there is no python or not from 5-6 languages Suppose I have an array named languages I am saying here let languages is equal to Suppose there is python Ok and there is javascript, c, c sharp I have all the languages I will say if if I will say languages.includes params.slug Then what should I do? Then return it Otherwise what should I do? Otherwise you will say post not found Ok So whether it is python, javascript java, cpp or c sharp I will make it cpp and cs If I write cs here Suppose this is available in my database My post will be cs If I write here harry Then post not found So this kind of logic can be used If we use dynamic routes Now in next js You can also make custom 404 page So for that what you have to do You have to make a page named not found.js So as soon as you make this page of not found.js Then what will happen? You will not see the page of 404 Suppose I do something like this I say any random thing So this page is showing me But I want this custom design page to show me What will I do? If I want custom design page to show me I will come here and make a file in my app And I will name it not found.js And here I have made a custom Not found page So I will refresh Let me see what's wrong Let me see what's wrong Restart the server And yes, our not found page is coming You can see I have styled it with tailwind.css And you can see Earlier our not found was like this Now it is like this So I can write anything I can write here slash blog post slash harry slash one slash two So you can see In a very similar way You can make an error.js So let me show you If you make a file in your app directory With the name error.js And you write error Suppose you want to write So whenever your error comes You will see that error page Now the issue here is We have to mark it as used client Because our error page This should be the client component I will copy the code here And you can see Whenever error comes Then what will happen Here your error will be shown in the console So I do one thing I try to bring an error So let's do one thing In our slug I bring an error I write here let a is equal to Or I simply do throw new error Throw new error Is I wrote it like this Now if I try to execute it Blog post slash harry So here you can see Something went wrong try again And you can see error And if I show you my console So here also console.log Because we have made an error You can see here uncut error And it has shown me Something went wrong page Which is error.js page Now you can use a reset function To recover from it It will try to re-render If it goes by re-rendering Then it's a good thing Your user can click on this Try again and try to recover from it So that was about Dynamic routing And error handling How you can make error pages Before going I want to tell you one more thing Assume that I make An end point here about And I do something like this And I write here I write anything I write val anything And here slash page.js So I want to show you How we have I will remove it How we have written this slug If I print params here If I Console.log params And my server is here If I come slash about slash something If I write slash about slash harry Here I write slash about slash harry So you can see What will happen here Why it is showing page not found Ok so here page not found Post not found is showing But you can see here in val it is showing harry I will do one thing I will remove all this I will remove all this I will remove all this And I will return A div and I will write I am about page check console Ok I will write this Now you can see what is in params Now you can see val harry What about if I write here slash about slash harry Slash one slash python Python spelling is wrong Please pardon that Now you can see here harry is also here One is also here and python is also here So if you want to Do some handling like In url there is not one parameter There are multiple parameters Like if you want slash blog post slash one slash two slash three And one two and three You want to take all these values You will make a dynamic route And if you do dot dot dot val You will see an array in val In which all three values are there In this case your harry one and python three I hope you understood How to use dynamic route In an advanced way And how to fully control Any dynamic route Of your endpoints How many ever endpoints I put here I can access from this page And I can control from here I can write logic here What will happen when I go to slash about slash harry What will happen when I go to slash about slash harry Slash about What will happen if I put many urls after slash about What to do with all those This will come here as val Params dot val And I will be able to handle it easily If you have not yet accessed the playlist of sigma Then do access it Because there are many good projects coming in it And I hope you will enjoy watching those projects That's all for now guys Thank you for watching this video And I will see you next time
